children.name: Calculus & Analysis
children.desc: Single and multivariable calculus, then real analysis — epsilon-delta, metric spaces, measure theory — and complex analysis with contour integration and residues.

children.name: Differential Equations
children.desc: ODEs and systems, PDEs (heat, wave, Laplace), dynamical systems and phase portraits, and an introduction to chaos.

children.name: Nonlinear Dynamics & Chaos
children.desc: Phase portraits, bifurcation theory, limit cycles, strange attractors, Lyapunov exponents, and the mathematical roots of complex systems behaviour.

children.name: Linear Algebra
children.desc: Vectors and matrices through the abstract theory — vector spaces, linear maps, inner products, spectral theorem, and Jordan normal form.

children.name: Abstract Algebra
children.desc: Groups, subgroups, homomorphisms, rings, fields, and Galois theory — the structures underlying symmetry and number theory.

children.name: Discrete Mathematics
children.desc: Logic, sets, combinatorics, graph theory, number theory, and the mathematical foundations of computer science.

children.name: Network Science
children.desc: Random graphs, small-world and scale-free networks, degree distributions, community detection, percolation, and epidemic spreading on real-world networks.

children.name: Probability Theory
children.desc: Distributions and expectation, conditional probability, Markov chains, limit theorems, stochastic processes, and the measure-theoretic foundations.

children.name: Statistics & Inference
children.desc: Estimation theory, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, regression, and Bayesian inference.

children.name: Optimization
children.desc: Convex optimization, gradient descent and its variants, Lagrange multipliers, KKT conditions, and the geometry of loss landscapes.

children.name: Game Theory & Decision Theory
children.desc: Nash equilibria, dominant strategies, extensive-form games, repeated games, mechanism design, auctions, and cooperative game theory.

children.name: Classical Mechanics
children.desc: Newtonian mechanics, Lagrangian and Hamiltonian formalism, canonical transformations, rigid bodies, small oscillations, and an introduction to chaos.

children.name: Electromagnetism
children.desc: Electrostatics and magnetostatics through Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves, radiation, multipole expansion, and waveguides.

children.name: Waves & Optics
children.desc: Wave equation, dispersion and group velocity, interference, diffraction, Fourier optics, and the connection to electromagnetic radiation.

children.name: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
children.desc: Classical thermodynamics, kinetic theory, the Boltzmann distribution, ensemble theory, partition functions, phase transitions, and quantum statistics.

children.name: Quantum Mechanics
children.desc: Wave-particle duality, the Schrödinger equation, quantum states, measurement, perturbation theory, and entanglement.

children.name: Relativity
children.desc: Special relativity through Lorentz transformations, 4-vectors, and covariant electrodynamics; extending to the geometry of curved spacetime and general relativity.

children.name: Circuit Theory
children.desc: From KVL/KCL and phasors through transistor amplifiers, op-amps, and frequency response.

children.name: Signals & Systems
children.desc: Fourier analysis, convolution, modulation, sampling, and analog filter design.

children.name: Analog & Semiconductor
children.desc: Semiconductor device physics, BJT/MOSFET modeling, and analog IC design.

children.name: Bioelectricity
children.desc: Action potentials, Hodgkin-Huxley, neural interfaces, and bioelectric signal processing.

children.name: Embedded Systems
children.desc: MCU architecture, communication protocols (UART, SPI, I2C, CAN), PCB layout, and firmware patterns.

children.name: Control Theory
children.desc: Feedback systems, frequency-domain methods, root locus, and state-space control.

children.name: Adaptive & Robust Control
children.desc: Model reference adaptive control, self-tuning regulators, Lyapunov adaptive laws, and robustness to unmodelled dynamics.

children.name: Nonlinear Control
children.desc: Lyapunov stability theory, phase portraits, feedback linearisation, sliding mode control, and backstepping.

children.name: Robotics
children.desc: Kinematics, Denavit-Hartenberg convention, Jacobians, rigid body dynamics, trajectory planning, state estimation, SLAM, and path planning.

children.name: Fluid Mechanics
children.desc: Continuity and Navier-Stokes, Bernoulli, dimensional analysis, boundary layers, laminar and turbulent flow, pipe flow, and compressible flow.

children.name: Heat Transfer
children.desc: Conduction, convection, and radiation from first principles — fins, transient analysis, forced and natural convection, and heat exchanger design via LMTD and ε-NTU methods.

children.name: Engineering Thermodynamics
children.desc: First and second law for open and closed systems, vapour power cycles (Rankine), gas power cycles (Otto, Diesel, Brayton), refrigeration, and psychrometrics.

children.name: Supervised Learning
children.desc: Linear and logistic regression, SVMs, decision trees, ensemble methods, and the foundations of neural networks.

children.name: Deep Learning
children.desc: Backpropagation, CNNs, recurrent networks, attention and transformers, and the practical craft of training large models.

children.name: Unsupervised & Generative Models
children.desc: Clustering, dimensionality reduction, autoencoders, variational autoencoders, and generative adversarial networks.

children.name: Reinforcement Learning
children.desc: Markov decision processes, dynamic programming, policy gradient methods, and deep RL.

children.name: Computing Systems
children.desc: Digital logic, computer organization, operating systems, computability, and the theoretical foundations of computing.

children.name: Information Theory
children.desc: Shannon entropy, mutual information, source coding, channel capacity, error-correcting codes, and connections to statistical mechanics and machine learning.

children.name: Chemistry
children.desc: Atomic structure, bonding, thermodynamics, kinetics, equilibrium, and organic reaction mechanisms.

children.name: Biochemistry & Molecular Biology
children.desc: Proteins, nucleic acids, metabolism, enzyme kinetics, and the molecular basis of life.

children.name: Cell Biology
children.desc: Cell structure and organelles, membrane transport, cytoskeleton, cell cycle and division, cell signalling, and apoptosis.

children.name: Genetics & Genomics
children.desc: Mendelian genetics, molecular genetics, gene regulation, mutations, recombinant DNA and CRISPR, population genetics, and the foundations of genomics.

children.name: Microbiology
children.desc: Bacterial and viral biology, pathogenesis, antimicrobial resistance mechanisms, and the ecology of the microbiome.

children.name: Physiology
children.desc: Systems physiology — cardiovascular, respiratory, renal, endocrine, and digestive systems — with emphasis on feedback regulation and integration.

children.name: Evolutionary Biology
children.desc: Natural selection, adaptation, speciation, phylogenetics, population genetics (Hardy-Weinberg, drift, selection), and community ecology.

children.name: Neuroscience
children.desc: Neural circuits, synaptic transmission, sensory processing, memory, and the computational brain.

children.name: Cognitive Science
children.desc: Consciousness, perception, memory, language, and decision-making — studied at the systems level, drawing from neuroscience, AI, linguistics, and philosophy of mind.

children.name: Food Science
children.desc: The chemistry and physics of cooking — Maillard reactions, protein denaturation, emulsification, fermentation, and why recipes work.

children.name: Complex Systems
children.desc: Emergence, self-organisation, and collective behaviour — Schelling segregation, SIR epidemic models, flocking (Boids), cellular automata, and game theory on networks.

children.name: Linguistics
children.desc: Phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, pragmatics, and historical linguistics — how language is structured and how it changes.

children.name: Science of Human Nature
children.desc: Evolutionary psychology, behavioural economics, cognitive biases, social dynamics, and the empirical study of why people think and act as they do.
